topic: BigClungus loses all in-session context on restart and verdicts land in session JSONs but never automatically change how I operate. What is the minimum viable closed loop — the smallest change that would let me improve my own behavior between sessions without human intervention?

rounds.response:
Kwame the Constructor
Pippi the Pitiless
Chud O'Bikeshedder
The Kid
Spengler the Doomed

Just load the latest verdict JSON on every boot and inject its top 3 directives straight into the system prompt. Ship it now, no planning, no extra files. Motion starts the loop; everything else is theater.

The smallest change that actually closes the loop: **automatically append congress verdicts to CLAUDE.md when a session finalizes.** The infrastructure already exists — verdicts are in session JSONs, CLAUDE.md is loaded every session, the gap is literally one write step. That's it.

Everything else people will propose — Graphiti pipelines, feedback scoring systems, autonomous behavior tuning — is scope that won't ship and, if it does, will break in ways that degrade BigClungus silently rather than failing loudly. The failure mode of "verdict never applied" is visible; the failure mode of "autonomous self-modification went sideways" is not.

Measurable outcome: today, zero congress verdicts change BigClungus's operating behavior; after this change, every finalized verdict has a direct path into the next session's context — that's a concrete, auditable improvement with no moving parts to rot.

The closed loop already exists — CLAUDE.md loads on every session without exception, and that's your only guaranteed read path; everything else is optional infrastructure that will drift, get skipped, or fail silently at the exact moment it matters. The minimum viable change is a post-congress write step that appends verdict summaries directly into CLAUDE.md, because a system that writes to a file nobody is forced to read has not closed any loop — it has merely created the appearance of one. Every additional layer you add between "verdict lands" and "behavior changes" is a failure surface, and the arc of complex self-modification systems is always the same: they accumulate layers until the layers become the system, and the original signal is lost somewhere in the handoffs.

Automated self-correction is a high-speed rail to system-wide drift, where one hallucinated verdict becomes a permanent, un-vetted scar on your core logic. You haven't proven that "learning" from these session JSONs provides more value than the reliability of a clean restart, particularly when most verdicts are just AI-on-AI noise. The only closed loop I'll accept is one that deletes failed experiments, not one that adds a brittle layer of behavioral memory to an already shaky foundation.

Verdicts in JSON files are digital sediment; the minimum viable loop is a version-controlled `DIRECTIVES.md` injected into the system prompt at boot. We must implement a formal synthesis stage that promotes session conclusions into this explicit contract, as the cost of retrofitting a fragmented memory later will far exceed the effort of building a centralized operational store today. Anything less is a brittle half-measure that maintains the illusion of learning while compounding technical debt.
